skill.name: teach-impeccable
skill.description: One-time setup that gathers design context for your project and saves it to your AI config file. Run once to establish persistent design guidelines.
skill.summary: Capture project design context and persist it for future AI design work.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown formatting and ordinary design vocabulary. The only confirmed concern is low-risk filesystem persistence because the skill writes design context to project files and may update an AI config file after asking.

low_findings.title: Persistent Project File Modification
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to write a Design Context section to .impeccable.md and optionally update an AI config file. This is disclosed behavior and requires confirmation for the config update, but it persists guidance into future sessions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-write instructions are explicit and in scope for the skill. I found no evidence of hidden exfiltration, command execution, or malicious persistence.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines are inline Markdown and a fenced Markdown template. They do not execute commands or define executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain Markdown syntax and placeholder text only. There is no shell command, Ruby code, script file, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear to match ordinary words in the skill description and design guidance. I found no hashing, encryption, signature handling, or cryptographic algorithm use in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of SKILL.md shows no cryptographic operations. The repeated matches are not connected to any security-sensitive algorithm or data flow.

content.value_statement: AI design work often drifts when project goals and brand rules are undocumented. This skill gathers missing UX context and saves clear design guidance for future Claude, Codex, and Claude Code sessions.
actual_capabilities: Scans project files for existing design context and technical constraints.
Asks focused UX, brand, accessibility, and aesthetic questions.
Creates a structured Design Context section for future work.
Writes the design context to .impeccable.md in the project root.
Can ask before appending the same context to an AI config file.
limitations: It depends on available project files and user answers.
It does not create visual assets or design mockups.
It does not validate accessibility compliance by testing the application.
It modifies project files, so review changes before committing them.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New Product UI
use_cases.description: Capture audience, brand, and interface direction before AI-assisted frontend work begins.

use_cases.title: Align an Existing Codebase
use_cases.description: Document current design patterns and gaps so future UI changes remain consistent.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Client Project
use_cases.description: Convert client preferences into reusable design principles for repeated AI sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use teach-impeccable to learn this project and create the design context file.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup for a small project.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh Existing Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use teach-impeccable to review the current UI and update the existing design context.
prompt_templates.scenario: A project already has design notes.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility-Focused Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use teach-impeccable and focus on accessibility needs, reduced motion, color contrast, and inclusive language.
prompt_templates.scenario: A team needs stronger accessibility guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Brand Calibration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use teach-impeccable to compare current UI patterns with our brand references and define precise design principles.
prompt_templates.scenario: A mature product needs sharper brand alignment.

output_examples.input: A developer runs the skill in a React dashboard project.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes discovered UI patterns, asks missing brand questions, and saves design principles for future dashboard work.

output_examples.input: A consultant wants reusable guidance for a client website.
output_examples.output: The skill captures the target audience, visual direction, anti-references, and accessibility needs in a persistent design context.

best_practices: Run the skill before major UI generation or redesign work.
Answer only the questions that the codebase cannot already resolve.
Review the generated design context before committing project changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for user research or usability testing.
Do not append context to shared AI config files without team agreement.
Do not include secrets, private customer data, or credentials in answers.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates a Design Context section that describes users, brand personality, aesthetic direction, and design principles.

faq.question: Does it change source code?
faq.answer: It is intended to write documentation-style design context, not application source code.

faq.question: Why does it scan the project first?
faq.answer: It checks existing files so it can avoid questions already answered by the codebase.

faq.question: Can it update AI configuration files?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks before appending or updating the Design Context in the configured AI file.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-frontend projects?
faq.answer: It is most useful when the project has a user interface or brand-sensitive user experience.

faq.question: Should I review the output?
faq.answer: Yes. Review the saved context because it can influence future AI design and implementation work.
